Name: info_theoretic_quantization
Title: Information-Theoretic Limits of Neural Network Quantization
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that the fundamental limits of neural network quantization can be characterized using information-theoretic measures, such as entropy and mutual information, and that these limits can be used to develop more efficient and effective quantization algorithms.
Related Work: Recent works such as EMQ, AMP-ViT, and SNIP have explored the use of mixed-precision quantization and adaptive precision allocation for neural networks. However, these works have focused primarily on empirical methods and have not fully explored the information-theoretic limits of quantization. In contrast, our proposal seeks to establish a theoretical foundation for neural network quantization using information-theoretic measures.
Abstract: Neural network quantization is a crucial technique for reducing the computational and memory requirements of deep learning models. However, the fundamental limits of quantization are not well understood. In this work, we propose to explore the information-theoretic limits of neural network quantization using measures such as entropy and mutual information. We will develop a theoretical framework for characterizing the trade-off between quantization error and information loss, and will use this framework to design more efficient and effective quantization algorithms. Our approach will be grounded in the principles of information theory and will provide a new perspective on the problem of neural network quantization.
Experiments: We will conduct experiments to validate our theoretical framework and to evaluate the performance of our quantization algorithms. Specifically, we will: (1) develop a suite of benchmark datasets and models to evaluate the performance of different quantization algorithms; (2) use information-theoretic measures to characterize the trade-off between quantization error and information loss for different quantization algorithms; and (3) design and evaluate new quantization algorithms that are optimized for information-theoretic criteria such as entropy and mutual information.
Risk Factors and Limitations: One potential risk of our approach is that the information-theoretic measures we use may not fully capture the complexities of neural network quantization. Additionally, our approach may require significant computational resources to evaluate the information-theoretic measures and to optimize the quantization algorithms. To mitigate these risks, we will use a combination of theoretical analysis and empirical evaluation to validate our approach, and will explore efficient algorithms and approximations to reduce the computational requirements.
